title: Minimum Number of Pushes to Type Word II
titleCn: 输入单词需要的最少按键次数 II

testcases.input: word = "abcde" Output: 5 Explanation: The remapped keypad given in the image provides the minimum cost. "a" -> one push on key 2 "b" -> one push on key 3 "c" -> one push on key 4 "d" -> one push on key 5 "e" -> one push on key 6 Total cost is 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 = 5. It can be shown that no other mapping can provide a lower cost.
testcases.expectedOutput: 5 Explanation: The remapped keypad given in the image provides the minimum cost. "a" -> one push on key 2 "b" -> one push on key 3 "c" -> one push on key 4 "d" -> one push on key 5 "e" -> one push on key 6 Total cost is 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 = 5. It can be shown that no other mapping can provide a lower cost.

testcases.input: word = "xyzxyzxyzxyz" Output: 12 Explanation: The remapped keypad given in the image provides the minimum cost. "x" -> one push on key 2 "y" -> one push on key 3 "z" -> one push on key 4 Total cost is 1 * 4 + 1 * 4 + 1 * 4 = 12 It can be shown that no other mapping can provide a lower cost. Note that the key 9 is not mapped to any letter: it is not necessary to map letters to every key, but to map all the letters.
testcases.expectedOutput: 12 Explanation: The remapped keypad given in the image provides the minimum cost. "x" -> one push on key 2 "y" -> one push on key 3 "z" -> one push on key 4 Total cost is 1 * 4 + 1 * 4 + 1 * 4 = 12 It can be shown that no other mapping can provide a lower cost. Note that the key 9 is not mapped to any letter: it is not necessary to map letters to every key, but to map all the letters.

testcases.input: word = "aabbccddeeffgghhiiiiii" Output: 24 Explanation: The remapped keypad given in the image provides the minimum cost. "a" -> one push on key 2 "b" -> one push on key 3 "c" -> one push on key 4 "d" -> one push on key 5 "e" -> one push on key 6 "f" -> one push on key 7 "g" -> one push on key 8 "h" -> two pushes on key 9 "i" -> one push on key 9 Total cost is 1 * 2 + 1 * 2 + 1 * 2 + 1 * 2 + 1 * 2 + 1 * 2 + 1 * 2 + 2 * 2 + 6 * 1 = 24. It can be shown that no other mapping can provide a lower cost.
testcases.expectedOutput: 24 Explanation: The remapped keypad given in the image provides the minimum cost. "a" -> one push on key 2 "b" -> one push on key 3 "c" -> one push on key 4 "d" -> one push on key 5 "e" -> one push on key 6 "f" -> one push on key 7 "g" -> one push on key 8 "h" -> two pushes on key 9 "i" -> one push on key 9 Total cost is 1 * 2 + 1 * 2 + 1 * 2 + 1 * 2 + 1 * 2 + 1 * 2 + 1 * 2 + 2 * 2 + 6 * 1 = 24. It can be shown that no other mapping can provide a lower cost.
